content.full_text:
State of California

Memorandum

To : File                                     Date : March 27, 1989


From : FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
      John McLean

Subject : T-89-019


     Spoke to Pamela Gibson in January.  She needed an immediate
answer.  I advised her that the conservative advice is that the
mailing is sent at public expense.  She indicated that she did not
have time for full written advice and would rely on this
conservative advice.

City of La Palma
CITY HALL - 7822 Walker Street, La Palma, California 90623    (714) 523-7700    Office of the
                                                                                    City Manager

December 28, 1988

Ms. Diane Griffiths, General Counsel
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA 95804

Dear Ms. Griffiths:

Our City Council, who also sits as the Redevelopment Commission, has
authorized participation in a Chamber of Commerce Factbook in the form of
a $1,500 full color ad.

While they will not have their names or pictures appear in the ad, the
Chamber would like to have their names and pictures in the section on
City Government.

My questions are as follows:

If they contribute as the Redevelopment Commission, can their pictures
(as Councilmembers) appear in the book?

If they choose not to contribute, can their pictures appear?

I realize you are swamped, but we need an answer right away. The
Chamber's consultant indicated he called the Fair Political Practices
Commission and was told it was perfectly all right. I have called and
been told it was not all right.

We need your assistance in sorting this out.

Sincerely,

Pamela Gibson
City Clerk

PG/prt

California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

January 5, 1989


Pamela Gibson
City Clerk
City Hall
7822 Walker Street
La Palma, CA 90623

Re: 89-019

Dear Ms. Gibson:

Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform
Act was received on January 3, 1989 by the Fair Political
Practices Commission. If you have any questions about your
advice request, you may contact John McLean, an attorney in the
Legal Division, directly at (916) 322-5901.

We try to answer all advice requests promptly. Therefore,
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions,
or more information is needed, you should expect a response
within 21 working days if your request seeks formal written
advice. If more information is needed, the person assigned to
prepare a response to your request will contact you shortly to
advise you as to information needed. If your request is for
informal assistance, we will answer it as quickly as we can.
(See Commission Regulation 18329 (2 Cal. Code of Regs. Sec.
18329).)

You also should be aware that your letter and our response
are public records which may be disclosed to the public upon
receipt of a proper request for disclosure.

Very truly yours,

Kathryn E. Donovan
Acting General Counsel

KED:ld


428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660
